zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Digges於大約1465年出生在英國肯特時，她的父親John Digges27歲，母親Joan Clifton17歲。她於大約1482年與Richard Sackville在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1524年5月24日之後。

nameMeanings.name: Digges
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Dicks , from the personal name Digge, a variant of Dick with voicing of the final consonant.

alternatively, a nickname from Middle English digge ‘duck’, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept, caught, or sold ducks or for someone thought to resemble a duck in some way.

variants.name: Diggs
meanings: English (London): variant of   Digges  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Dicks
meanings: English: variant of Dick , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
North German and Dutch: genitive patronymic from the personal name Dick . In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the much more common Dutch variant Diks.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Digges was born about 1465, in Kent, England, her father, John Digges, was 27 and her mother, Joan Clifton, was 17. She married Richard Sackville about 1482, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 9 daughters. She died after 24 May 1524.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Digges est née environ 1465, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, John Digges, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan Clifton, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Sackville environ 1482, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée après 24 mai 1524.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Digges(이)가 대략 1465년 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Digges(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Clifton(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1482년 영국에서 Richard Sackville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1524년 5월 24일 이후 사망했다.
